challenges.prompt: The user bumps their browser's base font size from 16px to 20px for accessibility. With the CSS below, what happens to the two headings on the page?
options: .title grows to 40px while .subtitle stays fixed at 32px
Both headings stay exactly 32px regardless of the user's setting
Both headings grow proportionally to the new 20px base
challenges.explanation: rem is relative to the root font size, which inherits the user's browser preference, so 2rem becomes 2 x 20px = 40px. px is an absolute unit that ignores that preference entirely, so 32px stays 32px. This is exactly why the lesson recommends rem for font sizes.

challenges.prompt: This code has a bug — the designer wanted a soft, washed-out (muted) blue, but instead they got a fully vivid blue. What's wrong?
options: To mute the color you must lower the saturation (the 2nd value), not leave it at 100% — 50% lightness is the most vivid form of the hue, not a grey
You must lower the lightness (the 3rd value) from 50% toward 0% to wash the color out
hsl() only produces muted colors when the hue is above 300, so 217 forces full vividness
challenges.explanation: In HSL the lightness scale runs black (0%) to vivid color (50%) to white (100%), so 50% lightness is the MOST saturated version of the hue, not a mid-grey. To get a muted, washed-out shade you drop the saturation (e.g. hsl(217, 30%, 65%)), leaving hue and lightness alone.

challenges.prompt: Complete the unit so this hero section fills the entire window height, no matter how tall the browser window is.
challenges.explanation: vh means 1% of the viewport height, so 100vh equals the full window height and the hero always fills the screen. Its sibling vw is 1% of the viewport width, which the lesson pairs with clamp() for fluid headlines.

challenges.prompt: Order these lines to build a working blue color scale: first open the :root block, define shades from lightest to darkest by only changing the HSL lightness, then close the block. Top line = first, bottom line = last.
challenges.explanation: Custom properties live inside a :root block that must open and close with braces. Locking the hue (217) and saturation (80%) and only lowering the lightness from 90% to 15% gives a cohesive light-to-dark tonal palette from a single source of truth — the killer feature of HSL.
